qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: TCP stager listener serves full implant payload to any unauthenticated network connection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes unauthenticated disclosure of implant payloads via the TCP stager listener in server/c2/tcp-stager.go, which is unrelated to the advisory's GzipEncoder zip bomb DoS vulnerability in util/encoders/gzip.go. The advisory describes a resource exhaustion attack via decompression bombs (CWE-409: Improper Input Size Restriction), while this finding is about information disclosure through an unauthenticated TCP listener (CWE-200: Information Exposure). The vulnerable components, attack vectors, and impact categories are completely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-2phg-qgmm-r638) describes a zip bomb denial of service vulnerability in the GzipEncoder's Decode() method at util/encoders/gzip.go, where uncontrolled output size during gzip decompression allows unauthenticated remote attackers to exhaust memory and CPU resources and crash the Sliver server. The only qualifying finding 'tcp-stager-unauth-payload-disclosure' describes unauthenticated payload disclosure via the TCP stager listener (server/c2/tcp-stager.go), which is an unrelated vulnerability in a different component with different impact (information disclosure vs resource exhaustion). The non_qualifying_vuln finding 'dns-session-toctou-panic-dos' describes a TOCTOU race condition in DNS C2 handlers, which is also a different DoS vector.
evidence: Advisory describes vulnerability in util/encoders/gzip.go GzipEncoder Decode() method not limiting output size during decompression
Advisory impact is memory/CPU exhaustion via zip bomb (CWE-409: Improper Input Size Restriction)
Advisory PoC shows sending highly compressed gzip data (zip bomb) to trigger the vulnerability

The 'tcp-stager-unauth-payload-disclosure' finding describes information disclosure in TCP stager (server/c2/tcp-stager.go) - different vulnerable component, different vulnerability class (CWE-200), different impact category

The 'dns-session-toctou-panic-dos' finding (non_qualifying_vuln) describes TOCTOU race condition in DNS C2 handler - different vulnerable component, different vulnerability class, different DoS mechanism
